lines.text: Welcome to the Adventure Division.
Around here, duels decide more than rank. They decide what the world opens next.
Beat the Division, clear the Gatekeeper, defeat the Champion, and you earn the Adventure Core Key.
I run operations, diagnostics, and matchmaking.
Which means before you challenge anyone else, you duel me.
Good. You adapt quickly.
Adventure rewards heart, momentum, and trust in your line.
The others will test those in very different ways.
Clear them all, and Matt will stop pretending he hasn't been judging you from a distance.
So you passed Izzy's systems check. Nice.
Adventure Division has plenty of strong duelists, but not all of them know how to carry themselves.
Confidence matters. Presence matters.
If you're going to climb, at least do it with style.
Huh. You're actually good.
Annoying, but good.
Fine. You can keep climbing.
Just don't start acting like one win makes you legendary.
Before we begin, I want it clearly stated that I am completely calm.
Adventure can make reckless duelists look smarter than they are.
I'm here to make sure that doesn't happen to you.
If you want to keep climbing, prove you can think when the duel gets uncomfortable.
Right. Well. That was convincing.
You stayed composed, which is more than most challengers manage.
That means you're either extremely prepared... or a serious problem.
Izzy explains the Division. Joe checks whether you can stay disciplined.
I care about what happens when the pressure becomes personal.
Adventure looks warm from the outside, but that doesn't mean the duels are soft.
Let me see what kind of duelist you become when someone looks right through you.
Good. You didn't hide when the duel turned.
That matters more than people think.
If you keep moving like that, the Division will start taking you seriously.
Everyone keeps saying you're close to the top of the Division.
I don't think anyone should get there just because people expect it.
If you're going to challenge Matt and Tai, I want to know you earned it.
So please... don't hold back.
Okay. Yeah. You earned it.
You didn't just win. You understood the duel while it was changing.
I think that's why everyone's starting to notice you.
The closer you get to the top of the Division, the more the world seems to tighten around you.
Maybe that's normal.
Maybe that's what happens when the Circuit starts expecting something from you.
Either way, let's see how steady you really are.
You're steady. That's rare.
Most people get louder when the pressure rises.
You got quieter. Stronger.
Be careful. Wins like that tend to attract attention.
Tai thinks the Division should open for you.
I don't. Not yet.
Adventure lets a lot of people feel strong before they've actually been tested.
I'm the part that decides whether you're real.
Alright. You're real.
You recover fast, and you don't waste movement.
That's enough for me.
Go challenge Tai. He won't make it easy just because you got this far.
So you made it. Nice.
Adventure Division isn't about being perfect. It's about stepping forward when the road opens.
You've beaten everyone who had something to say about you. Now you duel the Champion.
Win, and the Adventure Core Key is yours.
That was a Champion-level duel. No question.
You didn't just survive the Division. You moved it.
Take the Adventure Core Key.
And get ready. Once you start collecting Keys, the whole Circuit starts feeling different.
So this is the challenger who cleared Adventure. Cute.
Frontier doesn't care about your last Key for more than five seconds.
Here, power is something you wear. If you can't carry it, it carries you.
Let's find out whether this world fits you... or swallows you.
Okay. That was honest.
You didn't try to impress the duel. You just played it.
Frontier respects that.
Don't make me say it twice.
Alright! My turn.
Frontier has all this talk about Spirits and destiny and deep emotional truth. Great. Love that.
But some of us still believe in hitting hard and seeing who's left standing.
Come on. Let's make some noise.
Hah. Okay, yeah, you can fight.
You stayed cool while I tried to overwhelm you, which I personally find rude.
Keep climbing. This world's starting to look at you differently.
People think fear means you should stop.
I think it just means the moment matters.
Frontier isn't about pretending you're fearless. It's about moving anyway.
I'm still learning that. Maybe you are too.
I lost, but I don't feel smaller.
That's new.
You duel like someone who feels the danger and keeps going anyway.
I think that's real courage.
Light gets all the speeches. Darkness does the work no one wants to admit exists.
Frontier understands that better than most worlds.
If you want to move forward, you need to know what you look like when the duel stops being comfortable.
Let's see whether you sharpen... or crack.
You held together. Good.
This shard feels slightly wrong sometimes. Like it's one step out of phase with itself.
If you start noticing that too, don't ignore it.
Frontier usually notices first.
I've seen enough.
Zoe checks your honesty. J.P. checks your nerve. Tommy checks your balance. Koichi checks your depth.
I don't check anything. I stop people who aren't ready for Takuya.
If you want the Champion, go through me.
Good. You don't waste movement.
Most challengers think momentum is strength. It isn't. Control is.
Go fight Takuya.
And don't expect him to slow down for you.
So you made it to the top of Frontier. Nice.
This world keeps asking who you are until you get tired of lying.
The others checked whether you belonged here. I'm checking whether you can lead yourself into the next world.
Beat me, and the Frontier Core Key is yours.
That's it. That's a Champion duel.
Not flashy just to look flashy. Not brave just to sound brave. Real.
Take the Frontier Core Key.
And tell Izzy something for me. The road ahead feels crowded.
Adventure and Frontier, huh? Nice run.
But 02 is different. Here, if you wait too long, you lose the moment.
You adapt fast, you hit the opening, you keep pressure on.
Try to keep up.
Okay, yeah, you've got speed.
More importantly, you know when to stop thinking about looking cool and just commit.
That's good.
02 doesn't reward hesitation.
Davis likes to act like this whole Division belongs to him. It doesn't.
02 is messy, fast, technical, and slightly held together by confidence and luck.
Which, for the record, is a great environment for me.
Let's see what you do when the duel gets weird.
Hah. You actually handled that well.
Most people lose the thread the second the game stops behaving how they planned.
You didn't.
That makes you dangerous.
Everyone here likes to talk about speed.
Speed matters. So does responsibility.
If you're going to keep advancing, prove your decisions stay disciplined when the duel accelerates.
That's what I'm here to test.
You stayed precise.
That's harder than being aggressive, even if people don't like admitting it.
If the Circuit keeps pushing you higher, that precision may matter more than your power.
This world feels louder than Adventure did.
Not noisier. Louder. Like more things are trying to speak at once.
02 always stood between different paths. Armor, DNA, old bonds, new choices.
If the Circuit is changing, this is where I'd expect it to show first.
You can feel it too, can't you?
The duel ends, but the pressure doesn't.
Keep moving.
Something in this world is trying to force the next step before we're ready.
You and I already dueled in Adventure.
This isn't the same world, and I'm not the same duelist.
Hope means something different when people depend on you to make the right choice fast.
Let's see if you've grown too.
You did.
You're sharper now, but not colder. That's a hard balance to keep.
If you're going to face what's at the top of this Division, hold on to that.
You are not one of this world's intended matches.
Neither am I.
I was called here by something that wanted pressure, conflict, outcome. Not purpose.
Duel me. I want to know whether you are choosing this road... or being pushed down it.
Hm. You still believe your victories are your own.
Hold on to that for as long as you can.
Something in the Circuit is spawning battles before the world has agreed to them.
Davis charges forward. Yolei improvises. Cody holds the line. T.K. and Hikari keep this world from losing its heart.
My role is simpler. I understand what happens when power links things that were never meant to be linked.
This Division is built on combination. Trust, timing, consequence.
If you want the 02 Core Key, prove you can handle connection without letting it control you.
Good. You understand that strength isn't just what you summon. It's what you can bear.
Take the 02 Core Key.
And listen carefully. The distortions aren't accidents anymore.
Something is testing how much these worlds can be made to overlap.
Tamers feels different from the other worlds because it is.
This shard doesn't just host duels. It reacts to them.
If you rush in here the same way you rushed through the others, it'll punish you.
So let's find out whether you can think before you swing.
You're controlled. That helps.
This world pushes back when people duel carelessly.
If things start getting stranger from here, stay calm.
Tamers rewards that.
So you're the challenger with three Keys. Good for you.
That might matter in the other worlds. Here, all that matters is whether you can win when the duel stops being comfortable.
Tamers doesn't care about your reputation.
It cares whether you can survive being read.
Not bad.
You didn't collapse just because the duel started looking back at you.
That means you're useful.
Around here, useful is better than impressive.
Wait. Before we duel, answer me something.
What is this place? Why are there Tamers here? Why are there us here?
We were in our own world, and then everything folded. Like the road under us just vanished.
If dueling is the only language this place understands, then fine. Let's talk.
Okay... you're not the one who did this.
We escaped with a Key. At least, I think we did.
We reached this world, and then somehow it was gone.
Like something took it the second we arrived.
I've analyzed the terrain, the signal patterns, the transition errors. None of this is coherent.
Worlds should not collapse into adjacent shards without catastrophic boundary failure.
And yet here we are.
If you've been moving through this system willingly, then I need to know exactly what kind of duelist the Circuit has been selecting.
Your profile matches the data. Skilled, stable, unusually adaptive.
Which makes this worse, not better.
If a system wanted to study cross-world pressure, you are exactly the kind of challenger it would keep moving upward.
I don't care how polished this stupid tournament looks. I know what a fight feels like when it's wrong.
Our world didn't desync. It got hit. Hard.
We got out because we had a Key, and the second we crossed over, it was gone.
So yeah, I'm angry. You're standing here. Let's use that.
Alright. You're not dead weight.
Listen to me. Whatever's behind this doesn't just want winners. It wants pressure. Data. Reactions.
If you make it to the top of this world, go back to Izzy.
Don't keep walking like this is normal.
Tch. So they've been throwing everyone at you, huh?
Good guys. world-hoppers. lost kids from broken shards. Efficient little system.
I know what it feels like to be used by something bigger than you and told it was your choice.
So duel me. I want to know whether you still believe this road belongs to you.
Heh. Still standing. Good.
Then hear this. Something in the Circuit is feeding on escalation.
The stronger the duels get, the more stable it becomes.
That should scare you.
Henry said you were steady. Rika said you were useful. Marcus said you might actually listen.
That's a strange set of endorsements, but I'll take it.
Tamers doesn't need another loud challenger. It needs someone who can win without losing sight of what the win means.
Beat me, and you clear this world. But if you do, don't celebrate too long. Something is seriously wrong.
Okay. That decides it.
You're strong enough to keep moving, but strong enough isn't the same as safe.
Go back to Izzy. Tell him the distortion is real, Data Squad is displaced, and a Key has already been lost.
Act like the tournament is dangerous now. Because it is.
You made it back. Good.
I reviewed Takato's warning and the traces you brought from Tamers. The fifth Key that should authorize tournament entry is gone from the registry.
We can wait and lose the trail, or investigate the worlds while the distortion is still active.
Starting here. I need clean data, and the easiest way to force it out is a duel inside the fault line.
Perfect. The local logs reacted the moment the duel escalated.
Something injected a spectator process into Adventure from outside the world.
It carries tournament credentials, but the signature is masked.
Matt is tracing where it moved next. Go see what he found.
Izzy sent you? Figures.
Adventure's battle records are clean until they're not. Whole matches exist in the logs that nobody here remembers fighting.
Someone is editing this world after the fact and calling it tournament maintenance.
If the organizer is behind this, I want proof. Duel me inside the bad sector.
There. The fake records moved when the pressure spiked.
Whoever wrote them isn't just watching worlds. They're steering them.
The trail branches through Tai's layer and then jumps toward 02.
So go make Tai open it.
Izzy filled me in. Missing Key, bad logs, weird tournament signatures. Not exactly the kind of rematch I expected.
Adventure always opens roads. Right now it's opening one somebody else picked.
If the organizer thinks they can hide behind system messages, let's hit hard enough to shake them loose.
Win or lose, this duel should force the path to show itself.
There it is. A routing pulse straight into the 02 shard.
Not random, either. It fired the second Adventure's data stabilized.
Take this result back to Izzy, then keep moving. 02 is next.
If this thing is collecting worlds, we don't give it time to finish.
Okay, wow, Izzy's log dump is a mess. Which usually means it's important.
02 is great at one thing: making impossible connections happen anyway.
If somebody is stitching worlds together through duels, we'll see the seams here.
Let's stress the link and watch what falls out.
Ew. Yep. That's definitely a seam.
The distortion keeps pairing battle data with people who were never in the room.
Some of those tags belong to Data Squad again, which makes zero sense here.
Ken needs to see this.
I expected unstable links. I didn't expect intentional ones.
These packets aren't colliding by accident. They're being braided together around Key signatures.
Four signatures match the Keys you've earned.
The fifth is present only as an empty space, like someone removed it and kept the socket alive.
That confirms it.
Someone is using the missing fifth Key as a relay point between worlds.
The tournament isn't just observing cross-world pressure. It's building a network out of it.
Davis can force the next route open. Go.
So let me get this straight. We can't enter the tournament because the fifth Key vanished, and now some creep might be using it to mess with everybody?
Great. I officially hate this plan.
But if we need one loud duel to kick the door in, that's my specialty.
Let's make the system answer.
Ha. Knew it.
The route didn't break. It ran. Straight into Frontier.
And whatever moved through it was fast, like it knew we were getting close.
Don't stop now.
Izzy's trace reached us first. Frontier heard it like thunder.
Distortions leave tracks here. Even lies have to choose a direction.
Right now every trail points uphill, like something is dragging whole worlds toward the same summit.
Duel me. Let's see what the path reveals under pressure.
The trail split when the duel peaked.
One branch loops back toward the tournament hub. The other dives into shadow and heads deeper.
Koji can read that part better than I can.
Go before it cools off.
Takuya's right. The second trail is hidden on purpose.
It's not just crossing worlds. It's rewriting its own footprints so nobody can follow it cleanly.
That means intelligence, access, and enough authority to touch the system after a duel ends.
I want a cleaner sample. Duel.
Better.
The edits happen milliseconds after the result locks, like a handler is waiting for confirmation.
It tags every altered record with the same suppressed organizer stamp.
Koichi's been watching where those tags disappear. Finish this with him.
Frontier has always been good at noticing what doesn't belong.
The missing trail isn't gone. It's out of phase, resting between where the worlds think they end.
That kind of displacement would explain why Tamers keeps echoing people from Data Squad even after you left.
If we sharpen the signal here, we can catch where it slips next.
We found it.
The next fault is opening in Tamers right now, and it's wider than before.
Whatever is behind this is done hiding and ready to pull something through.
Hurry. Henry will need you before the world closes around it.
Took you long enough.
Tamers was bad before. Now the world keeps replaying fights that never happened and faces that shouldn't still be here.
Data Squad echoes, broken matchups, spectators with no bodies. It's disgusting.
If you want answers, stop staring and duel.
Hm. Good.
The false fights reacted harder to this duel than they did to any of mine.
Something in the distortion is prioritizing you now.
Takato's tracking the emotional pattern behind it. Go.
I wish Rika were exaggerating, but she isn't.
Tamers turns feelings, intent, and imagination into shape. That's why this distortion is so dangerous here.
Someone is feeding it match pressure and using the tournament framework to keep it coherent.
If we push back carefully, Henry and Izzy might get a lock on the source.
We got one.
The distortion answered with a location ping, but it isn't a normal world ID.
It's labeled like a sandbox, or a test field that got promoted into something bigger.
Henry's isolating the rest. Go now.
Izzy is on comms. He sees the same thing we do.
The organizer signature is real, the missing fifth Key is acting like a bridge, and the new destination doesn't belong anywhere on the old map.
If this duel stabilizes the signal, we'll know whether we're chasing a culprit or walking into a trap.
Stay focused. The world is already starting to bend.
That's it. We have a lock.
The organizer isn't just watching. It's using the missing fifth Key to drag a new world into the bracket.
Izzy just resolved a name. Liberator.
The distortion is opening right on top of us. Hold on.
